content: Read all 7 files of cto/6df5bc78-66d3-4cfb-9ce4-3b912bc47411 directly (00-deps.json, 00-frame.json, disposition.json read this cycle; 01-plan/make/check/reflect.jsonl were read in the prior session and are re-confirmed present, not re-quoted here in full). Confirmed the trail's own frame matches the three-item true_intent it was dispatched with.

Read cto/skills/domain-scope/SKILL.md directly. Its Owns section states verbatim: 'Architecture design, PMCR-O loop/skill-pack design and validation, security posture, DevOps pipelines, incident response.' Editing orchestrator-role/SKILL.md and adding brief-session.md under the Platform-tier orchestrator package is PMCR-O loop/skill-pack design and validation -- squarely in cto's Owns, not a Law 4 crossing into another domain's territory.

Independently re-read the live orchestrator-role/SKILL.md in full. Confirmed zero '../../' substrings remain, the six prior '../../' locations now read as full repo-relative paths (catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/{references,scripts}/...), 'Usage (System Contract)' heading present, no 'Invocation Contract' string anywhere, and no prose outside the path substitutions differs from what the prior trail's own diff claimed. Independently re-read brief-session.md's frontmatter and confirmed it matches sibling commands/replay-trail.md and commands/seal-trail.md's shape (description key, fenced parameter block, prose sections).

Re-evaluated cto/6df5bc78's self-seal of 'accept' against hil-gating.md's TYPE1 list: no write to catalog/skills.json or either marketplace.json twin, no rename/removal of an existing catalog entry, no git commit/push executed as part of that cycle itself (the commit happened in a later, separate session action, after the trail had already sealed). None of the enumerated TYPE1 triggers fired during the cycle itself. The human's dispatch ('/orchestrator:run-cycle cto "Hardening & Narrator: 1. ... 2. ... 3. ..."') named the exact file, the exact section rename to confirm, and the exact new command file to create -- concrete and specific enough to match the pre-authorization pattern already established by trail cto/67f24fd5's own precedent-setting accept. Agree the self-seal was correctly reasoned.

Ran 'git show --stat cba3315' directly (human-approved, read-only) rather than trusting the prior session's own report of what it staged and committed. Output confirms exactly two files changed: commands/brief-session.md (new, 98 insertions) and orchestrator-role/SKILL.md (33 changes, +/-). No unrelated file swept into the commit. Commit message correctly references trail cto/6df5bc78-66d3-4cfb-9ce4-3b912bc47411.

Noted, not blocking: this commit (cba3315) is local to main and has not been pushed to origin as of this cycle -- confirmed via the same git session state, not assumed. Whether to push, and whether to bring .pmcro/trails/ under version control, remain open human decisions this cycle does not resolve on the initiative's behalf.
